story.iWant: my text edits to be automatically saved
story.soThat: I don't lose changes when I close the browser

task.title: Implement localStorage Auto-Save Script
task.subtask: Verify existing auto-save script in slide HTML (from 3.2/3.3 templates)
Ensure blur event listener attached to all contenteditable elements
Capture all contenteditable content on blur
Save to localStorage with key slide-edits
Include debounced input listener for periodic saves during typing

task.title: Implement State File Persistence
task.subtask: Design slide-state.json schema
Add file sync mechanism from localStorage to slide-state.json
Implement periodic sync (every 5 seconds or on page unload)
Ensure selector uses [data-field='fieldName'] format
Add lastModified ISO 8601 timestamp

task.title: Implement Edit Restoration on Load
task.subtask: On page load, check localStorage first for immediate restore
If localStorage empty, load from slide-state.json (fallback)
For each edit, find element by selector and set innerHTML
Handle case where selector doesn't match
Log restoration success/failures to console

task.title: Ensure Performance Requirements
task.subtask: Verify localStorage save completes within 100ms
Ensure file sync doesn't block UI (async operation)
Test with multiple edits in rapid succession
Measure total save time from blur to localStorage persistence

task.title: Cross-Browser Testing
task.subtask: Test auto-save in Chrome
Test auto-save in Firefox
Test auto-save in Safari
Verify localStorage behavior consistent across browsers
Run Frontend Test Gate checklist

task.title: Edge Case Handling
task.subtask: Handle localStorage disabled (warn user)
Handle corrupted localStorage data (clear and restart)
Handle very long text content (no truncation)
Handle special characters in content (proper escaping)

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Given a slide is open in the browser, when the user edits text via contenteditable, then changes are captured on blur event
State file includes: selector (data-field attribute value), content (edited text), lastModified timestamp
Given the user reopens the slide later, when the slide loads, then saved edits are applied from state file
Text auto-save occurs within 1 second of edit completion (NFR3)

doc.title: Slide Builder PRD
doc.snippet: FR32: All text elements in slides are editable via contenteditable. FR33: Text edits are auto-saved to slide state file.

doc.title: Slide Builder PRD
doc.snippet: NFR3: Text auto-save occurs within 1 second of edit completion.

doc.title: Slide Builder Architecture
doc.snippet: State file tracks edits with selector (data-field) and content. On layout regeneration: read state, generate new layout, apply saved edits to matching selectors.

doc.title: Slide Builder Architecture
doc.snippet: .slide-builder/single/slide-state.json tracks text edit state per slide with edits array containing selector and content.

doc.title: Epic 3 Tech Spec
doc.snippet: Edit Tracker module: JavaScript embedded in slide HTML, listens for blur events on contenteditable elements, saves to localStorage immediately, syncs to slide-state.json periodically.

file.reason: Existing auto-save script implementation - already captures contenteditable content on blur, saves to localStorage with slide-edits key, and restores on page load
Template for auto-save script that must be included in all generated slides - defines the saveEdits function, blur/input event listeners, and restore logic
Template file that should include auto-save script - verify script is present
Template file that should include auto-save script - verify script is present
Template file that should include auto-save script - verify script is present
Template file that should include auto-save script - verify script is present
Template file that should include auto-save script - verify script is present
Template file that should include auto-save script - verify script is present

node.name: puppeteer
node.purpose: HTML-to-image conversion for export (not directly used by this story)

node.name: googleapis
node.purpose: Google Slides API for export (not directly used by this story)

browser.name: localStorage API
browser.purpose: Primary persistence mechanism for immediate auto-save in browser

constraint.rule: localStorage is the primary persistence layer for immediate saves (browser-native, instant)
slide-state.json is the secondary persistence layer synced from localStorage
File:// protocol prevents direct JavaScript file system writes - requires external sync mechanism
All text elements must have contenteditable="true" and unique data-field attributes
Selector format must be [data-field='fieldName'] for state file
NFR3: Text auto-save must complete within 1 second of edit completion
Must work in Chrome, Firefox, and Safari (NFR18)

interface.name: saveEdits Function
interface.signature: const saveEdits = () => { /* collect all contenteditable, save to localStorage */ }

standards.paragraph: This project uses manual browser testing via Frontend Test Gates. Each story has a test gate with prerequisites, step-by-step user actions, expected results, and success criteria checkboxes. Tests are performed in Chrome, Firefox, and Safari browsers. Performance testing measures time from blur event to localStorage persistence. Edge cases include localStorage disabled, corrupted data, and special characters.

testIdea.description: Edit title text, blur, verify localStorage slide-edits key contains edited title
Edit multiple elements sequentially, verify all captured in localStorage
After editing and blurring, verify slide-state.json file exists in .slide-builder/single/
testIdea.note: Requires external sync mechanism since file:// can't write directly

testIdea.description: Verify slide-state.json structure: selector uses data-field format, content matches edited text, lastModified is valid ISO 8601
Edit text, close browser completely, reopen slide, verify edits restored
Edit text, refresh page (Cmd+R), verify edits restored immediately
Time from blur event to localStorage.setItem completion - must be < 1 second
Rapid successive edits (10 edits in 5 seconds), verify all saved without loss
Disable localStorage in browser settings, verify graceful degradation with console warning
Corrupt slide-edits localStorage value manually, verify recovery on page load
Edit text with special characters (<script>, quotes, unicode), verify proper escaping
